summary.headline: ADM reports fourth quarter and full-year 2024 results with adjusted EPS of $4.74 and announces $500-$750 million in cost savings.
summary.narrative_summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (ADM) reported fourth quarter and full-year 2024 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share of $4.74 for the year. The company noted a decline in earnings and operating profits across most segments, particularly in Ag Services and Oilseeds. In response to challenging market conditions, ADM announced targeted actions to deliver $500-$750 million in cost savings over several years. Additionally, the company increased its quarterly dividend by 2%.

drivers: higher origination volumes and margins in North America
higher destination marketing volumes and margins in Global Trade
concerns: lower executed crush margins in North America
higher imports of used cooking oil
softer demand from food customers in North America
lower co-product values and margins in EMEA
higher industry production and inventory levels

extraction_notes: Numbers extracted from Exhibit 99.1; no guidance changes or facility mentions in the filing.
